lemonde:
LIVE, war in the Middle East: Donald Trump demands “unconditional surrender” from Iran; South Beirut and Tehran bombed. 
The American president intends to be involved in the choice of future Iranian leaders and in the future reconstruction of the country. Israel carried out numerous bombings on the southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital and announced, early in the afternoon, that it was launching a new raid on Tehran.

In Dordogne, the era of wars and rearmament revives the century-old powder mill of Bergerac. 
The increase in world conflicts has revived the city's arms factory, created during the Great War and today owned by a public company. The activity revitalizes the economy of a long-stricken sub-prefecture.

Trial of Tariq Ramadan for rape: an arrest warrant issued by France. 
The preacher is in Switzerland and has still not appeared for his trial which opened on Monday.

LIVE, war in Ukraine: after a skirmish between Ukraine and Hungary, the EU deplores “unacceptable” threats from Volodymyr Zelensky against Viktor Orban. 
“We hope that one person in the EU will not block the 90 billion. Otherwise, we will give the address of this person to our armed forces,” the Ukrainian president said, referring to the Hungarian prime minister who is blocking the 90 billion euro loan that Brussels wants to pay to Kiev.

Municipal elections in Romans-sur-Isère: the shadow of the Crépol drama hangs over the election. 
The electoral campaign in this city of 33,000 inhabitants is taking place in a heavy atmosphere. The outgoing mayor was unable to allay fears and resentments after the murder of a teenager in Crépol in 2023, seen as a symptom of an alleged ethnic clash on the national political and media scene.

Infant milk consumed by a dead baby in Angers contained an “abnormal” dose of cereulide toxin, according to the family’s lawyers. 
It still remains “to establish that this situation medically explains the mechanism of death” of the infant, the lawyers said.

Municipal: culture, an issue relegated to second place in the campaign. 
Until now preserved, this sector is becoming a budgetary adjustment variable for municipalities, whose finances are under pressure.

“The endless war in Ukraine is becoming a major problem for Russia, and Europeans must seize the historic moment.” 
In a column in “Le Monde”, political scientist Marie Mendras calls for urgently strengthening European support for Kiev, in particular by accelerating arms deliveries, setting up an air shield and increasing the number of civil and military missions.

The signs engraved on this figurine, more than 32,000 years old, would form a proto-writing. 
Should we see the rudiments of writing in the series of notches engraved in this delicate human silhouette, found in a cave in Germany? The question divides experts.

Municipal in Guadeloupe: Olivier Serva, the local ambitions of an ex-macronist. 
The deputy for the 1st constituency of Guadeloupe is a candidate for the third time for mayor of Abymes, the largest municipality in the archipelago.

In Venezuela, inflation soared to 475% in 2025, according to the country’s central bank. 
At the end of last year, economists estimated that Venezuela was on the threshold of hyperinflation, an episode that the Caribbean country had already experienced between 2017 and 2022.

Series break: France in the dark mirror of “A Prophet” and “Traqués”. 
Released almost simultaneously, the two fictions depict a country overwhelmed by violence, fragmented into an infinity of identities.

Jacques-Pierre Gougeon, former diplomat: “The deep unease between France and Germany risks fragmenting the European Union.” 
In a column in “Le Monde”, the academic explores the mechanisms at work in the disintegration of Franco-German relations and worries about the consequences of this diplomatic estrangement.

“We also see sex scenes filmed”: when Meta glasses subcontractors in Nairobi see everything, really everything. 
Two Swedish media outlets have revealed that employees of a Meta subcontractor in Kenya are viewing images captured by “Ray-Ban Meta” smart glasses, including intimate scenes. A complaint was filed in the United States.

In Switzerland, the tradition of “laundry rooms” in a cycle of tensions. 
In a country which favors individual responsibility, collective laundry rooms in buildings are nevertheless very widespread and give rise to numerous clashes, sometimes violent, between tenants.

In Gaza, medical evacuations interrupted since the start of the war in Iran. 
In the Palestinian enclave, evacuations of patients, timidly relaunched at the beginning of February, are suspended again because of the war in Iran, Israel having closed the Rafah border crossing for “security” reasons.

2026 Paralympic Games: “Make the Milan-Cortina Games a springboard for the success of the 2030 French Alps”. 
Andrew Parsons, president of the International Paralympic Committee, and Marie-Amélie Le Fur, patron of the French Paralympic and Sports Committee, discuss for “Le Monde” the importance of the Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympic Games which begin on Friday and will run until March 15.
